agents_page.title: Agents
agents_page.subtitle: Registered Hermes instances. Each agent points at a base URL and model — create many, switch from the top-bar AgentSwitcher.
agents_page.help_page: An agent = a named Hermes instance that Corey routes chats through. base_url is usually a local Hermes gateway (127.0.0.1:8642) or an OpenAI-compatible provider endpoint. The 'New agent' wizard pre-fills the common provider templates so you don't have to look any of that up. Every agent here registers with adapter id 'hermes:<id>' and appears in the top-bar switcher.

chat_page.title: Chat
chat_page.subtitle: Streaming chat with Hermes
chat_page.empty_sessions: No sessions yet. Start chatting to create one.
chat_page.empty_adapter_sessions: No {{adapter}} sessions. Switch to “All agents” to see others.
chat_page.retry_title: Regenerate the response using the same prompt. This replaces the current reply in place.
chat_page.export_title: Download this conversation as Markdown or JSON.
chat_page.export_disabled_hint: Nothing to export yet — send a message first.
chat_page.vision_warning: The current model {{model}} does not accept images. Switch to a vision-capable model or send text-only.
chat_page.message_placeholder: Message Hermes…  (Enter to send, Shift+Enter for newline)
chat_page.model_overridden_title: This session has a custom model override. Click to change or clear.
chat_page.model_default_title: Using the global default model. Click to pick a different model for this session.
chat_page.model_picker_empty: No models reported by the adapter. Configure a provider in Settings → LLMs.
chat_page.hero_subtitle_prefix: Connected through the local gateway on
chat_page.hero_suggestion_1: Explain what Hermes Agent is in one sentence.
chat_page.hero_suggestion_2: Summarize the TRAE sandboxing model in 3 bullets.
chat_page.hero_suggestion_3: Generate a basic Rust Tokio example for me.
chat_page.attach_vision_unverified: (image support for this model is unverified)

models_page.title: Language models
models_page.subtitle: The LLM backing the Hermes agent — configured in ~/.hermes/config.yaml
models_page.help_page: Pick which LLM Corey sends chat turns to. Any OpenAI-compatible provider works — OpenAI, Anthropic (via gateway), DeepSeek, Google, local Ollama. The provider dropdown pre-fills the base URL + API key env name; override either if your setup differs. Save writes ~/.hermes/config.yaml; Hermes needs a gateway restart to pick up the change. Individual chats can override this default from the chat composer.
models_page.change_model_desc: Pick a provider, set the model id, and save. The gateway must be restarted for changes to take effect.
models_page.custom_provider_warning: Custom provider slug. Make sure Hermes recognizes it.
models_page.model_hint_default: The exact id the provider accepts.
models_page.restart_desc: Hermes doesn't hot-reload config.yaml. Click below, or run `hermes gateway restart` manually.
models_page.profiles_desc: Reusable model configurations. Each profile is a named {provider, base_url, model, api_key_env} bundle you can reference from multiple agents.
models_page.profiles_empty: No profiles yet. Click "New LLM" to add one.
models_page.profile_field_id_hint: Auto-generated from the display name. Edit if you need a specific slug.
models_page.profile_field_model_hint: Type to filter or pick from this provider's common models.
models_page.profile_field_provider_placeholder: Pick or type a provider slug
models_page.profile_field_api_key_hint: Optional. If set, written to ~/.hermes/.env under the env var above.
models_page.profile_err_id_long: Id must be 32 characters or fewer.
models_page.profile_err_id_chars: Only lowercase letters, digits, '-' and '_' are allowed.
models_page.profile_err_id_duplicate: A profile with id "{{id}}" already exists.
models_page.missing_env_hint: — add it so Hermes can talk to this provider.

runbooks.title: Runbooks
runbooks.subtitle: Named prompt templates with placeholders. Invoke from the command palette or this page.
runbooks.help_page: A runbook is a reusable prompt with {{param}} placeholders. Good for anything you say often — 'summarize this URL', 'write a commit message from diff', 'translate to zh'. Click Use on a runbook, fill in the placeholders, and it drops into the chat composer ready to send. Scope a runbook to a specific Hermes profile to keep it out of the palette when you're working in a different context.
runbooks.import_title: Import runbooks from a JSON file
runbooks.import_empty: No runbooks found in the file.
runbooks.empty_desc: Create one to reuse a prompt with named placeholders across sessions.
runbooks.no_params_to_fill: This runbook has no parameters — launches the template as-is.

field.name: Name
field.template_hint: — wrap placeholders in double curly braces

scope.no_active_profile: No active Hermes profile — showing universal runbooks only
scope.empty_visible_desc: Toggle "Show all scopes" above, or create a universal runbook.

skills.title: Skills
skills.subtitle: Browse, edit, and save Hermes skill files under ~/.hermes/skills/.
skills.help_page: A skill is a Markdown file with YAML frontmatter that describes a repeatable task — 'deploy the staging build', 'summarise a paper', 'write a commit message'. Hermes loads every SKILL.md under ~/.hermes/skills/ and lets the agent invoke them by name. Local tab = skills you authored yourself. Hub tab = wraps the `hermes skills` CLI to browse and install from 7+ community sources (official, skills-sh, clawhub, …).
skills.pick_title: Pick a skill on the left
skills.pick_desc: Or click New skill to start one. Files are plain Markdown; Hermes reads them on the next run.
skills.empty_tree: No skills yet. Create one with the New button in the header.
skills.history_desc: Every save snapshots the prior body. Up to 50 versions are kept per skill; older ones are evicted automatically.
skills.history_empty: No prior versions yet. Save an edit to start building history.
skills.history_restore_title: Overwrite the current body with this version. The current version is snapshotted first, so this is reversible.

field.path_hint: Relative to ~/.hermes/skills/. Nested paths like work/standup.md are fine.

terminal.title: Terminal
terminal.subtitle: A native PTY-backed shell in your browser. Multi-tab, full UTF-8, resize-aware.
terminal.help_page: A real shell (your login shell with its actual environment) running inside Corey. Uses portable-pty so pipes, job control, and curses apps all work. Multi-tab so you can have a tail -f and a REPL side by side. Common use: peek at ~/.hermes/logs/, poke at files the agent just wrote, or drive other CLIs without switching apps.
terminal.open_hint: Open a terminal to get started.

trajectory.title: Trajectory
trajectory.subtitle: Visualise a past session as a timeline of messages and tool calls.
trajectory.help_page: Inspector view for one session. Each row is a message or tool call with timing, tokens, and a preview. Click a row to open the full payload. When the agent delegates a subtask (via Hermes' delegate_task tool), the subagent's tool calls are grouped as children of the delegation — use this to debug planner/executor chains.
trajectory.empty_desc: Send a chat turn and come back — each session renders here with a tool-call timeline.
trajectory.session_empty_desc: This session has no messages yet.

budgets.title: Budgets
budgets.help_page: Set spend caps per model / per Hermes profile / per adapter. CoreyOS tracks per-message tokens and estimates cost from a flat pricing table (100c / 300c per 1M tokens in/out). At 80% you get a warning badge; at 100%, send is blocked until the period rolls over. Windows are rolling — daily / weekly / monthly from the budget creation date, not calendar boundaries.
budgets.subtitle: Cap spend per scope, period, and breach action. Projection uses local usage history and a built-in price table.
budgets.breached: Over budget — action will fire on the next send.
budgets.empty_desc: Add one to see your projected spend against a cap. All storage is local — no cloud sync.

home.title: Welcome to Corey
home.subtitle: A control plane for AI agents — beautiful, fast, and not locked to one.
home.feature_compare: Send one prompt to multiple models side by side
home.feature_trajectory: Visualize session history and tool calls
home.feature_budgets: Track token spend and set cost caps
home.gateway_offline: Hermes gateway offline — click to configure
home.quick_1: Explain what Hermes Agent is in one sentence.
home.quick_2: Summarize the TRAE sandboxing model in 3 bullets.
home.quick_3: Generate a basic Rust Tokio example for me.
home.step_gateway_desc: Make sure the local Hermes gateway is reachable. If you haven't installed Hermes yet, run the Hermes setup from hermes-agent.nousresearch.com.
home.step_model_desc: Choose which LLM Corey should use by default. Any OpenAI-compatible provider works — OpenAI, Anthropic, DeepSeek, local Ollama, and more.
home.step_chat_desc: Send a prompt. Sessions are saved locally; you can revisit them from the sidebar.
home.step_profile_desc: Add a line or two to your user memory so the agent knows how to address you and which tech stack to default to.
home.step_channel_desc: Telegram, Discord, Slack, WeCom, WeiXin, Feishu — talk to the agent from your phone.
home.preset_desc: Drops 5 generic skills + a fetch MCP server + memory templates into ~/.hermes/. Safe to re-run; won't overwrite edits.
home.install_desc: Corey talks to a local Hermes gateway. Run the install command below, then click "I've installed it".
home.start_desc: We found the Hermes binary but the gateway isn't listening. Click Start to launch it.

agent_wizard.title: Add a new agent
agent_wizard.pick_provider: Pick a provider. We'll pre-fill the endpoint, API-key env var, and a starter model list.
agent_wizard.use_existing_desc: Pick one of your saved LLM profiles — no need to re-enter the API key. The agent inherits the profile's provider, base URL, and model.
agent_wizard.or_new_from_provider: Or start fresh from a provider
agent_wizard.field_id_hint: Lowercase letters / digits / '-' '_'. Auto-generated; edit if you want.
agent_wizard.api_key_docs: Where to get an {{provider}} API key ↗
agent_wizard.local_hint: No API key needed. Start the local backend first —
agent_wizard.use_profile_fresh_hint: Saving will also add this to your LLM profiles library.
agent_wizard.use_profile_linked_hint: Agent will inherit base URL, key, and model from this profile.
agent_wizard.err_id_long: Agent id must be 32 characters or fewer.
agent_wizard.err_id_chars: Only lowercase letters, digits, '-' and '_' are allowed.
agent_wizard.err_id_duplicate: An agent with id "{{id}}" already exists.

analytics.title: Analytics
analytics.subtitle: Usage rolled up from your local session history.
analytics.help_page: Single-shot rollup of your local SQLite session history. Shows sessions / messages / tool calls / active days / tokens, per-day activity, and the model + tool + adapter breakdowns. Everything is local — no cloud sync, no telemetry. Numbers only move when you actually chat; refresh re-queries the DB.
analytics.empty: No activity yet — start a conversation on the Chat page and come back here.

tokens.title: Tokens (last 30 days)
tokens.subtitle: Prompt + completion tokens per day.
tokens.empty: No token usage recorded yet — send a chat turn to start tracking.

feedback.title: Reply feedback
feedback.subtitle: Lifetime 👍 / 👎 ratings left on assistant replies.
feedback.empty: No ratings yet — hover an assistant reply on the Chat page to rate it.

settings.title: Settings
settings.help_page: Global preferences + Hermes connection. Language, theme, and shortcuts are local to this install. Gateway URL + API key + default model connect CoreyOS to your local Hermes gateway (usually http://127.0.0.1:8642). Hermes Instances section below lets you register additional OpenAI-compatible endpoints (self-hosted LLMs, remote Hermes, etc.). Sandbox Scopes let you partition which folders each agent can read/write to.
settings.subtitle: Preferences · gateway · storage

appearance.title: Appearance
appearance.desc: Visual preferences stored locally on this machine.

gateway.title: Gateway
gateway.desc: Where Corey sends chat requests. Changes take effect immediately — no restart needed.
gateway.api_key_hint: Optional. Matches the gateway's API_SERVER_KEY. Stored locally in plaintext.
gateway.api_key_placeholder: (leave empty if the gateway is unauthenticated)

settings.browser_llm_desc: Configure the AI model for browser automation. Stagehand needs an LLM to understand web pages and perform actions. Only token costs apply — DeepSeek (cheapest) or local Ollama (free) recommended.

routing_rules.title: Routing rules
routing_rules.desc: When the composed message matches a rule, the next turn is sent to the rule's target adapter instead of whichever one is active. First enabled match in list order wins; an empty list means routing is off.
routing_rules.empty: No rules yet. Add one to route /code, /translate, or other prefixes to the right adapter automatically.
routing_rules.field_id_hint: 1–64 chars: lowercase letters, digits, '-' or '_'. Frozen after save.
routing_rules.ai_empty: Not enough conversation data to generate suggestions yet.

hermes_instances.title: Extra Hermes instances
hermes_instances.desc: Register additional Hermes gateways so you can switch between them in the AgentSwitcher. Each entry is registered as 'hermes:<id>' alongside the primary gateway above.
hermes_instances.empty: Add your first agent to start chatting. Each agent connects to a Hermes gateway or API provider.
hermes_instances.field_id_hint: 1–32 chars: lowercase letters, digits, '-' or '_'. Frozen after save (used as the adapter id).
hermes_instances.field_default_model_hint: Override the model for chats routed through this instance. Suggestions come from the matching provider.
hermes_instances.field_sandbox_scope_hint: Per-agent filesystem isolation. Falls back to the default scope if unset.
hermes_instances.confirm_delete: Delete instance "{{id}}"? Its sessions remain in history but can no longer send new turns.

sandbox_scopes.title: Sandbox scopes
sandbox_scopes.desc: Named groups of workspace roots. Assign one to a Hermes instance to restrict what that agent's IPC-originated file ops can touch.
sandbox_scopes.confirm_delete: Delete scope "{{id}}"? Any instance pointed at it will fall back to the default scope.

model.title: Default language model
model.desc: Which LLM the Hermes agent uses when a chat doesn't specify one. Individual chats can override this from the composer.
model.label: Model id

storage.title: Storage
storage.desc: Where your local data lives on disk. Back these up or delete them manually.

sandbox.title: File sandbox
sandbox.desc: Control which directories the app can reach. Corey's disk operations are confined to workspace roots; sensitive system paths (e.g. ~/.ssh) are always denied.
sandbox.mode_dev_allow_hint: First launch. Everything outside the denylist is allowed. Adding a root flips to enforced mode.
sandbox.mode_enforced_hint: Only paths inside an approved workspace root can be touched.
sandbox.test_guide: To test: ask the agent to read a file outside your configured roots — it should be blocked and prompt for consent. Files inside roots should work normally.
sandbox.dev_hint: Sandbox is in developer mode — all file access is allowed. Click "Enforce now" to activate protection.

logs.title: Logs
logs.subtitle: Agent runtime logs, gateway tail, errors, and the config-change history.
logs.help_page: Four tabs unified under one page. Agent / Gateway / Errors tail ~/.hermes/logs/*.log with a substring filter. Changelog shows every config edit Corey journaled (model switches, API key rotations, profile changes) with a one-click Revert button. Revert is non-destructive — it writes the previous value as a new journaled change; nothing is ever lost.
logs.empty_desc: When you edit the Hermes model or API keys, each change lands here with a Revert button.
logs.not_revertible_env: API key values are never stored, so a deletion can't be undone. Re-add the key manually.

compare.title: Compare models
compare.subtitle: Run one prompt against up to 4 models in parallel. Each lane streams independently.
compare.help_page: Fire the same prompt at up to 4 models simultaneously. Each lane streams independently — you'll see tokens arrive as they come, not all at once. Lanes are cancellable individually. Export the result as Markdown or JSON to save a comparison. Good for: provider shootouts, reasoning-model vs fast-model trade-offs, prompt sensitivity checks.
compare.prompt_placeholder: Type a prompt, then Ctrl/⌘+Enter or Run. Multi-line is fine.
compare.empty_title: Pick at least one model and type a prompt
compare.empty_desc: Each selected model runs the same prompt in parallel. Lanes appear here with streaming output, per-lane latency, and winner highlights.

save_as_skill.disabled_hint: Send at least one message and wait for a reply first.
save_as_skill.title: Save this conversation as a Skill
save_as_skill.subtitle: Writes ~/.hermes/skills/<name>.md so Hermes picks the skill up on its next turn. Edit the body below to distil what should be reusable.
save_as_skill.name: Skill name
save_as_skill.body_hint: Frontmatter + full transcript pre-filled. Trim freely before saving.
save_as_skill.distill_hint: Ask the active model to rewrite the transcript as a proper SKILL.md.
save_as_skill.distill_empty: The model returned an empty body. Leaving the existing draft intact.

skill_hub.cli_missing_desc: The Hermes command-line tool is required to browse and install skills from the hub. Install Hermes Agent from https://hermes-agent.nousresearch.com/ and restart Corey.
skill_hub.source_hint: 7 federated sources: official Hermes skills, skills-sh, GitHub, Claw Hub, LobeHub, Claude Marketplace, and well-known directories.
skill_hub.install_hint: Full slug including source prefix, e.g. official/security/1password

mcp.title: MCP servers
mcp.subtitle: Model Context Protocol servers Hermes can talk to. Entries live under mcp_servers: in ~/.hermes/config.yaml — Hermes forks stdio commands and connects HTTP URLs itself.
mcp.confirm_delete: Delete MCP server "{{id}}"? Hermes will stop loading its tools on next reload.
mcp.restart_hint: MCP config changed. Hermes needs a reload before the new tools show up.
mcp.empty_desc: Add an MCP server to expose its tools to Hermes. Try the filesystem server first: npx -y @modelcontextprotocol/server-filesystem /path/to/project.
mcp.form_id_locked: Ids are frozen after creation — rename by deleting and re-adding.
mcp.transport_stdio_hint: Hermes forks the command. Use for npm / uvx / cargo MCP servers.
mcp.transport_url_hint: Hermes connects over HTTP. Use for hosted MCP servers.
mcp.form_template_hint: Fills the id + transport + config. Edit freely afterwards.
mcp.form_nous_bundled_hint: Nous Portal subscribers already have web search built-in via `hermes model` (Firecrawl). You may not need to add this MCP server.
mcp.form_error_id_dots: Id cannot contain '.' — it would be mis-interpreted as a nested YAML path.
mcp.form_error_must_be_object: Config must be a JSON object.
mcp.help_id: A stable identifier used as the key under mcp_servers: in config.yaml. Hermes refers to servers by this name in logs and tool errors. Cannot be changed after creation — to rename, delete and re-add.
mcp.help_transport: stdio: Hermes forks a local command (npm / uvx / cargo) and talks over its stdin/stdout. Use for tools that need local filesystem or OS access. url: Hermes connects via HTTP to a hosted server. Use for managed services like Stripe, GitHub, etc.

knowledge.title: Knowledge Base
knowledge.subtitle: Upload documents to build a searchable knowledge base. Relevant chunks are injected into chat context automatically.
knowledge.help: Upload text files (.txt, .md, .json, .csv, .yaml, source code). Documents are split into ~500-char chunks and indexed for Jaccard similarity search. When you send a chat message, the top relevant chunks are injected as system context. Future: embedding-based semantic search via Hermes gateway.
knowledge.drop_hint: Drop a file here or click Upload
knowledge.empty_desc: Upload a text file to build your knowledge base. Documents are automatically chunked and indexed.
knowledge.binary_warn: "{{name}}" is a .{{ext}} file. Binary formats may not extract correctly as plain text. Continue?
knowledge.size_warn: "{{name}}" is {{sizeKB}}KB. Large files may be truncated. Continue?

voice.title: Voice
voice.subtitle: Configure push-to-talk ASR and TTS playback. Supports OpenAI, Zhipu (智谱), and Groq providers.
voice.asr_desc: Hold the configured hotkey to record, release to transcribe and send.
voice.tts_desc: Assistant replies can be read aloud. Click the speaker icon on any message.
voice.tts_not_available: TTS is not available for this provider. Switch to OpenAI, Zhipu, or Edge TTS.
voice.hotkey_hint: Key combination to hold for push-to-talk (e.g. Meta+Space, Ctrl+Shift+V).
voice.endpoint_hint: Auto-filled after selecting a provider. Only change if using a self-hosted or proxy service.
voice.test_asr_hint: Click to start recording, speak, then click stop.
voice.mic_unavailable: Microphone recording is not available in this environment. On macOS, the WebView may not support getUserMedia. Try opening http://localhost:5173 in a browser, or wait for a future update with system-level recording support.
voice.error_playback: Playback failed: audio format not supported or file not found
voice.audit_desc: Every voice request is logged here so you can audit what data left your machine.

memory.title: Memory
memory.subtitle: Edit the notes Hermes injects into every prompt. Agent memory lives in ~/.hermes/MEMORY.md; user profile in ~/.hermes/USER.md.
memory.search_hint: Full-text search over Hermes' session database (~/.hermes/state.db). Supports FTS5 syntax.
memory.search_idle: Type a query and press Enter to search past sessions.
memory.new_file_hint: New file — first save will create it
memory.help_page: Agent memory (MEMORY.md) is what Hermes remembers across conversations — agent-authored notes about running tasks, gotchas, and project context. User profile (USER.md) is your identity and stable preferences (name, tech stack, communication style). Both files are injected into every system prompt automatically. 256 KiB cap keeps them sub-budget for long context windows.

channels.title: Platform channels
channels.subtitle: Credentials + behavior for every messenger Hermes can talk through.
channels.verified_title: Shipping end-to-end smoke test covers this channel.
channels.help_page: Each card is one messenger platform Hermes can chat through. Fill in the platform's credentials (bot tokens, webhook URLs, etc.), save, then restart the Hermes gateway to start serving that channel. Hermes handles the protocol per platform; CoreyOS is just the configuration surface. Verified badges indicate the channel has an end-to-end smoke test in CI.
channels.not_hot_reloadable: This channel is not hot-reloaded — you'll need to restart the gateway for the change to take effect.
channels.restart_prompt: Saved. Restart the Hermes gateway so the change takes effect now?

weixin.hint_account_id: WeiXin account identifier (the iLink bot account).
weixin.hint_token: iLink access token paired with the account ID.
weixin.hint_base_url: Overrides the default https://ilinkai.weixin.qq.com endpoint. Leave blank unless you're on a forked deployment.
weixin.hint_dm_policy: DM policy: open | allowlist | pairing | disabled. Defaults to 'open'.
weixin.hint_group_policy: Group policy: open | allowlist | disabled. Defaults to 'disabled'.
weixin.hint_allowed_users: Comma-separated WeiXin user IDs allowed to talk to the bot.

whatsapp.hint_enabled: Set to 'true' to enable the WhatsApp bridge process.
whatsapp.hint_mode: 'bot' (standard bot) or 'self-chat' (route to your own WhatsApp account).
whatsapp.hint_allowed_users: Comma-separated phone numbers (+<country><number>) allowed to talk to the bot.
whatsapp.hint_allow_all: Set to 'true' to skip the allowlist (dangerous on public deployments).
whatsapp.mention_patterns: Regex patterns that count as a mention

card_desc.telegram: Telegram group/DM bot. Easiest to configure, most common.
card_desc.discord: Discord server bot. Good for community groups.
card_desc.slack: Slack workspace bot (Socket Mode, needs Bot + App tokens).
card_desc.whatsapp: WhatsApp bridge (unofficial API, requires a separate bridge process).
card_desc.feishu: Feishu/Lark bot. Create an app in the Feishu Open Platform.

discord.hint_token: Discord Developer Portal → Bot → Copy Token.

slack.hint_token: Slack App settings → OAuth & Permissions → Bot User OAuth Token (xoxb-…).
slack.hint_app_token: App-level token (xapp-…) required for Socket Mode. Both tokens are needed for the standard Hermes Slack setup.

matrix.dm_mention_thread: Thread DMs when the bot is @-mentioned

feishu.hint_app_secret: Paired with App ID; keep secret.

wecom.hint_websocket_url: Optional override for the WeCom websocket endpoint. Defaults to wss://openws.work.weixin.qq.com.
wecom.hint_allowed_users: Comma-separated WeCom user IDs allowed to talk to the bot.

profiles.title: Profiles
profiles.subtitle: Each profile is a self-contained Hermes config under ~/.hermes/profiles/.
profiles.help_page: A profile is a named bundle of Hermes config — model, API keys, channel credentials, sandbox scope. Keep separate profiles for 'prod', 'dev', 'demo', 'work', 'personal'. Switch with one click; Hermes gateway restarts automatically so the new profile takes effect. Export a profile as .tar.gz to hand off or back up.
profiles.missing_desc: Hermes hasn't created this yet. Expected at: {{path}}
profiles.empty_desc: Create one to keep separate configs for prod, dev, or a demo account.
profiles.confirm_delete: Delete profile "{{name}}"? This removes its directory on disk.
profiles.cant_delete_active: Switch to a different profile first.
profiles.import_overwrite_warn: A profile named "{{name}}" already exists. Continuing will replace it on disk.
profiles.activate_confirm_fresh: Set "{{to}}" as the active Hermes profile?
profiles.activate_restart_gateway: Also restart the Hermes gateway so the change takes effect immediately.

hermes_logs.missing_desc: Hermes hasn't written to this log. Expected at: {{path}}
hermes_logs.follow_title: Auto-refresh every 2s while scrolled to the bottom

chat.title: No conversations yet
chat.desc: Start a chat session to see it here. Ctrl+Enter sends, Esc cancels a stream.

compare.title: Compare models side by side
compare.desc: Send one prompt to multiple models at once. Coming in Phase 4.

skills.title: Skills will live here
skills.desc: Browse, edit, and version skills. Coming in Phase 4.

trajectory.title: Session trajectory
trajectory.desc: Visualize turns, tool calls, and costs as a timeline. Coming in Phase 4.

analytics.title: Usage analytics
analytics.desc: Token usage, costs, daily trend, model mix. Coming in Phase 2.

logs.title: Agent logs
logs.desc: Tail agent / gateway / error logs with filters. Coming in Phase 2.

terminal.title: Web terminal
terminal.desc: Multi-tab PTY into the agent environment. Coming in Phase 4.

scheduler.title: Scheduled jobs
scheduler.desc: Manage cron jobs with natural-language task descriptions. Coming in Phase 2.

channels.title: Platform channels
channels.desc: Configure Telegram, Discord, Slack and 5 more. Coming in Phase 3.

models.title: Model providers
models.desc: Discover and manage models from your credential pool. Coming in Phase 2.

settings.title: Settings
settings.desc: Display, agent, memory, privacy, and server settings. Coming in Phase 2.

scheduler_page.title: Scheduled jobs
scheduler_page.subtitle: Run a prompt on a cron schedule. Each fire hits the Hermes adapter with your prompt as a single turn; the reply is stored only in the run history (no new chat session).
scheduler_page.help_page: Jobs run inside Hermes — we just write ~/.hermes/cron/jobs.json and Hermes' gateway picks them up. Cron syntax is the standard 6-field format (sec min hour day month weekday) — use 0 9 * * * * for 9am daily. Each run's output lands as a Markdown file under ~/.hermes/cron/output/{job_id}/ which you can browse via the Runs drawer.
scheduler_page.empty_desc: Create one to run a prompt on a schedule. Use any 6-field cron expression — see the hint below the cron field.
scheduler_page.field_cron: Cron expression (sec min hour day month weekday)
scheduler_page.field_prompt_placeholder: Summarize yesterday's standup notes and list today's blockers.
scheduler_page.cron_hint: Example: '0 0 9 * * *' = every day at 09:00. Use '0 */5 * * * *' for every 5 minutes. Hermes also accepts 'every 2h', '30m', or ISO timestamps.
scheduler_page.runs_empty: No runs yet. Hermes will create them when the job fires.

consent.title: Access permission required
consent.desc: The app is trying to reach the path below, which sits outside your current workspace. Choose how to proceed:

workflow_page.title: Workflows
workflow_page.subtitle: Define multi-step, multi-agent automated workflows
workflow_page.empty_desc: Create YAML files in ~/.hermes/workflows/ to define workflows
workflow_page.generate_dialog_title: Describe what you want to automate
workflow_page.generate_placeholder: e.g. Every morning at 9, fetch top 10 V2EX hot posts, ask GPT for a 3-bullet summary, and send it to my Telegram.
workflow_page.generate_hint: The AI assistant uses your default LLM. Generated workflows open in the editor for review before saving.
workflow_page.help_tip: Click 'Add Step' to create nodes, drag to connect dependencies, click a node to edit.
workflow_page.browser_profile_hint: Profiles with the same name share cookies and login state. Login once, reuse automatically.
